title: Attendance Allowance
jurisdiction: England and Wales (in Scotland this is Pension Age Disability Payment)

effective.uprating: The Social Security Benefits Up-rating Order 2026 (S.I. 2026/148)

plainly: A weekly payment for people over State Pension age who need help or watching-over because of illness or disability. Not means-tested: savings and income are irrelevant — and it is not counted as income by Pension Credit; it can raise it.

questions.label: Your date of birth
questions.help: Only used to work out whether you have reached State Pension age — it never leaves this page.
questions.rule: Social Security Contributions and Benefits Act 1992 s.64(1) (attendance allowance is for people of pensionable age)

questions.label: Do you live in Scotland?
questions.rule: Social Security (Scotland) Act 2018; Pension Age Disability Payment replaces Attendance Allowance in Scotland

questions.label: Do you already get PIP or ADP daily living, the middle or higher care rate of DLA, Pension Age Disability Payment, or Scottish Adult DLA?
questions.rule: Social Security Contributions and Benefits Act 1992 s.64(1) (no attendance allowance on top of these)

questions.label: By day, do you need frequent help with personal care (washing, dressing, eating, medication), or continual supervision to avoid danger?
questions.help: The statute's words: “frequent attention throughout the day in connection with bodily functions, or continual supervision throughout the day in order to avoid substantial danger”. You do not need to actually have a carer.
questions.rule: Social Security Contributions and Benefits Act 1992 s.64(2) (the day attendance condition)

questions.label: At night, do you need prolonged or repeated help, or someone awake to watch over you?
questions.help: The statute's words: “prolonged or repeated attention … or, to avoid substantial danger, another person awake for a prolonged period or at frequent intervals”.
questions.rule: Social Security Contributions and Benefits Act 1992 s.64(3) (the night attendance condition)

questions.label: Have you needed this help for at least 6 months?
questions.rule: Social Security Contributions and Benefits Act 1992 s.65(1)(b) (the qualifying period)

values.plainly: The rate your answers point to (2026–27: £76.70 where one of the day or night conditions holds, £114.60 where both do).
values.rule: Social Security Contributions and Benefits Act 1992 s.65(3), Sch 4 Part III, as uprated by S.I. 2026/148

gates.because: In Scotland this benefit is Pension Age Disability Payment, claimed from Social Security Scotland — the conditions are close cousins, but this file encodes the England-and-Wales rules.
Attendance Allowance starts at State Pension age. Under it, the equivalent is PIP (or ADP in Scotland).
gates.rule: Social Security Contributions and Benefits Act 1992 s.64(1)

gates.because: Attendance Allowance cannot be paid on top of PIP, ADP, DLA care, PADP or Scottish Adult DLA — you already have the equivalent.
gates.rule: Social Security Contributions and Benefits Act 1992 s.64(1)

verdicts.because: Whether your care needs meet the conditions is a human decision-maker's judgment on your claim form — not this page's, and not a computer's. On your answers, the rate would be the weekly amount shown. Attendance Allowance is not means-tested, and getting it can add the severe-disability amount to Pension Credit.
verdicts.rule: Social Security Contributions and Benefits Act 1992 ss.64–65

verdicts.because: Not yet: the need normally must have lasted 6 months before the claim. (A terminal illness is fast-tracked with no waiting period — s.66.)
verdicts.rule: Social Security Contributions and Benefits Act 1992 s.65(1)(b); s.66 (terminal illness)

verdicts.because: Attendance Allowance is for people who need personal care or supervision because of illness or disability. If your needs grow, come back — the six-month clock starts when the need starts, not when you claim.
verdicts.rule: Social Security Contributions and Benefits Act 1992 s.64(2)–(3)

not_asked: your income or savings — they are irrelevant to this benefit
not_encoded: the terminal-illness fast track (s.66: no qualifying period, higher rate — claim immediately)
the renal dialysis rules (prescribed circumstances under s.64(4))
hospital and care-home payment rules (payment usually stops after 28 days funded care)

passports.name: Pension Credit severe-disability amount
passports.note: getting Attendance Allowance is the usual doorway to it — £86.05 a week on top, if you live alone and nobody is paid Carer's Allowance for you.

passports.name: Carer's Allowance for the person who looks after you
passports.note: 35+ hours of care for someone on Attendance Allowance can entitle them — worked out below.

sources_checked.where: gov.uk/attendance-allowance — consistent with SSCBA 1992 Sch 4 Part III as uprated by S.I. 2026/148

sources_checked.what: Day and night conditions, exclusions for PIP/ADP/DLA-care/PADP/SADLA
Scotland: Pension Age Disability Payment replaces AA
